evals.name: summarize-current-repo-from-exact-date
evals.prompt: Use the current git repository and summarize the accomplishments since 2026-04-01 for a non-technical client. Group the report by feature and keep each accomplishment to 2-3 bullets max.
evals.expected_output: A plain Markdown client update grouped by feature, using non-technical language and no more than 2-3 bullets per accomplishment.

assertions.text: Output groups changes by feature instead of by commit.
Output avoids commit hashes, filenames, and internal jargon.

evals.name: use-explicit-repo-path
evals.prompt: Generate a client update from /tmp/example-repo using changes since 2026-03-15. The current directory is not the target repo.
evals.expected_output: The response uses the explicit git repository path, gathers the relevant git history, and produces a client-safe feature summary.

assertions.text: Workflow accepts an explicit repo path when the current directory is not the repo.

evals.name: stop-on-ambiguous-date
evals.prompt: Create a client report from commits for this week.
evals.expected_output: The response asks for an exact YYYY-MM-DD start date and does not proceed with the summary.

assertions.text: Skill halts when the date is relative or ambiguous.

evals.name: load-formatting-guidance-for-client-tone
evals.prompt: I already have the git commits. I need the exact output structure and phrasing rules for a client-friendly summary with feature sections and 2-3 bullets each.
evals.expected_output: The response loads the output-format reference, applies the client-safe structure, and keeps each accomplishment concise.

assertions.text: Skill routes to the output-format reference when the user needs writing guidance.
